title: Hubble Telescope celebrates 36th anniversary with gorgeous new image of famous Trifid Nebula
summary: The Hubble Telescope marked its 36th anniversary by releasing a stunning new image of the Trifid Nebula. The telescope has conducted over 1.7 million observations since its launch in 1990.

title: Sun unleashes 2 colossal X-flares within 7 hours of each other, knocking out radio signals on Earth
summary: The Sun emitted two massive X-class solar flares within 7 hours, causing temporary radio blackouts on Earth's dayside. These eruptions disrupted high-frequency radio communications.

title: Ted Cruz pushes back on NASA budget cuts: 'I don't want to wake up one day and look up at the moon and realize the Chinese have beat us there'
summary: Senator Ted Cruz criticized proposed NASA budget cuts, warning that the U.S. could lose its lead in lunar exploration to China. He reassured NASA's Artemis program engineers that political support would be maintained despite funding reductions.

title: Could 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' restore the 'Star Wars' spark? Watch the electrifying final trailer and decide if this is the way
summary: The latest trailer for 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' has reignited excitement for the Star Wars franchise. Fans are encouraged to watch and decide if it lives up to the hype.

title: What will happen when our sun starts dying? These 'stellar archaeologists' may have found a clue
summary: Scientists acting as 'stellar archaeologists' have discovered fossilized magnetism on dead white dwarf stars, potentially revealing insights into stellar evolution. This finding could help explain how stars, including our sun, transform over time.

title: These 'interstellar glaciers' could give water to young star systems. Could they support alien life, too?
summary: NASA's SPHEREx space telescope discovered widespread water ice in the Cygnus X star-forming region, suggesting these interstellar glaciers could supply water to young star systems. The findings raise questions about whether such environments could support alien life.

title: Ancient volcanic ash seen blowing across Mars in new spacecraft images
summary: New images from the Mars Express spacecraft reveal ancient volcanic ash spreading across Mars. This discovery provides insights into the planet's geological history and atmospheric conditions.

title: James Webb Space Telescope peers into a dying star surrounded by mysterious buckyballs: 'The structures we're seeing now are breathtaking'
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured detailed images of planetary nebula Tc 1, revealing new insights into the aftermath of a sun-like star's death and the presence of mysterious buckyballs.

title: Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS formed in a world much colder than the solar system
summary: Comet 3I/ATLAS, an interstellar visitor, originated in a colder environment than our solar system. This comet carries unique 'fossils' from its distant past, offering insights into its origin.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship's Block 3 variant at the Cape, aiming for a launch this year. The first flight of Block 3 is anticipated as the company advances toward this milestone.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impact on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: A busy week in the space industry is expected with seven missions launching from four countries using five different launch vehicles. The missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's plans to deploy a space-based cellular broadband network.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable orbital launch vehicles.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an Astronaut Flight Suit and a Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This innovation supports the development of a commercial space station.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures aboard the International Space Station in March. The month was marked by significant activity for the crew.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent CRS NG-23 mission completion.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, though the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. It highlights the significance of this milestone for the space industry.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or new methods for directional control. It highlights the significance of these innovations for mission success and efficiency.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's 86th parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers could effectively propel these ultralight materials, with potential applications in steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to improve scalability and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to target the 'Golden Dome' market.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Former President Trump has appointed two space industry executives to key military space roles. The nominees require Senate confirmation before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs. The article is published by Payload Pro.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has secured €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, arguing that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet presidential directives. The statement comes as a response to a recent budget proposal that has raised concerns among legislators.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships, suggesting that the Navy could assist in tracking satellites from the sea. It highlights a potential reciprocal relationship between satellite tracking and naval operations.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has secured €25.7 million in a Series A funding round to advance its commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space cargo services. The company plans to transition to regular operational service with its ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3 platforms.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is a missile defense program aimed at protecting the US. Companies are competing to secure contracts for this ambitious initiative.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under its CLPS initiative, creating opportunities for various space industry companies beyond just lunar-focused firms.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to establish ground infrastructure for its Meridian constellation by deploying teleports at Equinx's data centers. This partnership aims to support SpinLaunch's satellite network with compact teleport sites.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by growth in IoT subscribers. CEO Matt Desch discussed plans for NTN direct launches and spectrum discussions.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: French startup Univity secured a €27 million ($32 million) Series A funding round to develop two demonstration satellites for a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: The European Union is drafting the EU Space Act, which has raised concerns among U.S. officials about potential overreach. The proposed act aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities within the EU.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Australian low-cost airline Jetstar Airways has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed across 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities in the Nordic region across the space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen regional space infrastructure and services.

title: FCC Grants AST SpaceMobile Commercial Authorization for Direct-to-Device Service
summary: The FCC has granted AST SpaceMobile commercial authorization to provide direct-to-device satellite services using spectrum from partner mobile network operators. This approval marks a significant step for the company's space-based cellular broadband network.

title: Eutelsat Signs New Broadcast Deals in Mexico and Caribbean
summary: Eutelsat has signed three new broadcast deals in Mexico and the Caribbean, partnering with Co-op Cable to expand direct-to-home (DTH) and connectivity services.

title: Space Capabilities Underpin Global Security Strategies
summary: The article discusses the importance of space capabilities in global security strategies, highlighting key insights from SATShow Week and upcoming Space Symposium.

title: Pentagon to Move Forward On AEP Upgrades for GPS, As DoD Cancels GPS OCX
summary: The Pentagon has decided to cancel the GPS Next Generation Operational Control System (OCX) developed by RTX and instead proceed with upgrades to the existing GPS ground system. The move aims to enhance the current infrastructure managed by Lockheed Martin.
